content.text:
<user_query>
front end deployment failed chore: forensic reconciliation of remaining calibration chat commits … #231
</user_query>

I’ll investigate this as a deployment-failure forensic task tied to `#231`. First I’m going to inspect the existing terminal logs and repo references to identify exactly what failed and which calibration chat commits still need reconciliation.

I found terminal evidence of a large calibration-focused PR history but not yet the exact deployment error. Next I’ll pull the local git history plus issue/PR context for `#231` to pinpoint what still needs reconciling.

I found the exact failing workflow run: `Build & Deploy BIQc` for commit `chore: forensic reconciliation of remaining calibration chat commits …` (run `23729957007`). I’m now pulling the run diagnostics to isolate the frontend deployment breakage and patch it.

The CI summary confirms the frontend image build fails specifically at `yarn build`. I’m extracting the failed-step log lines now to pinpoint the exact source file and error message so I can fix it precisely.

I’ve confirmed the failing deployment was on a newer `origin/main` commit (`6d383e9`) than this worktree’s current HEAD. I’m going to switch the working tree onto that exact commit context so I can repair the broken frontend files directly and verify with a local build.

Switching to the failing `origin/main` snapshot is blocked by an existing local edit in `package-lock.json`. I’ll temporarily stash that file only, apply/verify the deployment fix on the failing commit, then restore your local lockfile change afterward.

I’m now on the exact failing commit and will surgically clean the merge artifacts causing duplicate declarations and top-level syntax breakage across the listed frontend files, then run a local frontend production build to verify.

I’ve isolated the breakage pattern: this commit has several accidental duplicate code blocks merged into the same files. I’m now applying a targeted cleanup pass (remove duplicate declarations/blocks, restore valid function boundaries/JSX) and then I’ll run `frontend` production build to confirm the deployment path is fixed.

I’m now cleaning the larger merge-collision files (`HomePage` and `useCalibrationState`) where partial duplicated blocks were spliced together mid-function. I’ll preserve the intended newer logic while removing the conflicting fragments that break parsing.
I’ve completed the first cleanup pass across all frontend syntax-failure files and I’m running a full `frontend` production build now to confirm we’ve actually eliminated the deployment blockers.
Local verification hit an environment gap (`craco` missing), so I’m installing frontend dependencies and then re-running the production build to validate the fix end-to-end.

I can’t run the exact CI environment locally because this machine is on Node 18 while the frontend lockfile now requires Node 20+. I’ll still force a local install with `--ignore-engines` to perform a best-effort syntax/build validation.

Frontend deployment failure was caused by merge-collision artifacts in the `#198` reconciliation commit (duplicate declarations + partially duplicated blocks), not infrastructure.

I forensically traced the failing run (`Build & Deploy BIQc`, run `23729957007`) and fixed the syntax breakages in these files:

- `frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js`
- `frontend/src/components/website/WebsiteLayout.js`
- `frontend/src/context/SupabaseAuthContext.js`
- `frontend/src/hooks/useCalibrationState.js`
- `frontend/src/lib/api.js`
- `frontend/src/pages/CalibrationQaAccess.js`
- `frontend/src/pages/MarketPage.js`
- `frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js`

What was fixed:
- Removed duplicated const/import blocks introduced by bad reconciliation.
- Removed unreachable duplicated return/call paths.
- Repaired broken function boundaries and catch blocks.
- Repaired malformed JSX branches and unclosed tags in summary sections.
- Cleaned duplicated hero/stat blocks in homepage so it parses and builds.

Validation:
- Ran `yarn build` in `frontend` successfully (production build completed).
- Build now compiles; only non-blocking ESLint warnings remain.

Notes:
- Local machine is Node 18, while deps/CI expect Node 20+, so I validated using `yarn install --ignore-engines` before build.
- I restored your prior local `package-lock.json` change after working on the failing commit snapshot.

If you want, I can next create a clean hotfix commit from these changes and prep a PR message tied to this deployment incident.
